rulings.Case Title: YING WANG VS. ANTHONY GO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Defendants' Attorney'S Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 10, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF YING WANG Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Defendants' Attorney'S Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to meet her burden to set aside the February 11, 2025 Judgment or the costs awarded in that Judgment, due to excusable neglect under CCP section 473(b). The Court finds that Plaintiff received proper notice of the Memorandum of Costs filed on December 9, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LINDA L. PERRY VS. JAMES A. PERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition, Appointment Of Appraiser And Broker Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Request For Judicial Notice; Declarations Of Scott Talkov, James Perry, Deborah Perry, Matthew Taylor
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 10, 2025 line 4. CROSS COMPLAINANT JAMES PERRY AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE DEBORAH AND JAMES PERRY REVOCABLE TRUST, DEBORAH PERRY MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition, Appointment Of Appraiser And Broker Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Request For Judicial Notice; Declarations Of Scott Talkov, James Perry, Deborah Perry, Matthew Taylor is GRANTED, in part. The Court orders a Partition by open-market sale of the property, pursuant to Section 874.320. The parties have 10 days after the entry of order to agree on a real estate broker licensed in the State of California to offer the property for sale. If the parties cannot come to an agreement, the court shall appoint the broker and establish a reasonable commission. The Court notes that Mr. Michael Bellings, the parties proposed listing agent, is not a broker. The Court will set a status conference on April 21, 2025 to appoint a broker if the parties cannot come to an agreement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
